content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

March 22, 1988

Mr. Harold S. Toppel
City Attorney
City of Saratoga
c/o Atkinson & Farasyn
P.O. Box 279
Mountain View, CA 94042

Re: Your Letter of March 14, 1988;
Our File No. G-88-112

Dear Mr. Toppel:

I am in receipt of your letter of March 14, 1988,
expressing concern regarding two advice summaries appearing in
the March 1988 issue of the FPPC Bulletin. I enclose copies of
the two advice letters for your review. (Advice Letters to
Gonzales, No. A-88-021; and Dillon, No. A-88-048.) You have
stated that the City of Saratoga publishes and distributes by
mail a city newsletter called "The Saratogan."

You have asked regarding the possible effects on the
publication of "The Saratogan" if the Commission adopts one of
the proposed revisions to 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18901. Specifically, you have asked whether the roster
of city officials which appears in "The Saratogan" will be
permissible if a listed elected official is currently a
candidate for election.

I enclose for your review and comment a copy of the four
alternative versions of the proposed revision to the
regulation. The Commission will consider the adoption of one
of the alternatives at its next meeting, April 5, 1988, in
Sacramento.

In light of your question regarding the roster type of
listing in "The Saratogan", I would direct your attention to
the following:

Alternative #1 - subdivision (c), especially (c)(3);

Alternative #2 - subdivision (c), especially (c)(2)(C);

Alternative #3 - subdivision (d), especially (d)(2)(C); and

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Harold S. Toppel
March 22, 1988
Page 2


Alternative #4 - subdivision (c), especially (c)(2)(C).

We look forward to the receipt of any comments you or your
client may wish to offer. If you have questions, I may be
reached at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Robert E. Leidigh
Counsel, Legal Division

Enclosure

sections.question: Whether the roster of city officials which appears in 'The Saratogan' will be permissible if a listed elected official is currently a candidate for election, in light of proposed revisions to 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18901.
sections.question_synthetic: The City Attorney asks whether a city newsletter's roster of city officials remains permissible under proposed regulatory revisions when a listed official is a candidate for election.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC directs the requestor to review specific subdivisions within four alternative versions of the proposed regulation revision, which will be considered at the April 5, 1988 Commission meeting, and invites comments on the alternatives.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a procedural/guidance letter rather than a formal advice letter with Q/C sections. The FPPC is directing attention to proposed regulatory alternatives rather than providing a definitive conclusion. Document appears twice in provided text (duplicate pages).

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether the roster of city officials which appears in 'The Saratogan' will be permissible if a listed elected official is currently a candidate for election, in light of proposed revisions to 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18901.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC directs the requestor to review specific subdivisions within four alternative versions of the proposed regulation revision, which will be considered at the April 5, 1988 Commission meeting, and invites comments on the alternatives.

embedding.summary: FPPC correspondence responding to the City of Saratoga's concerns about how proposed revisions to Section 18901 will affect the publication of an official roster in the city newsletter when listed officials are candidates for election.
